characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Co-vice principal of North Jackson High, Gamby is chief disciplinarian of the school. He has allied with his nemesis and fellow vice principal, Lee Russell, to take down new principal Dr. Belinda Brown. Gamby’s ultimate goal is to get the job himself. A Lighter Shade of Black: Gamby would be much harder to sympathize with if it weren't for Russell being so much worse of a person than him. Anti-Villain: Gamby is selfish, stupid and narrow-minded, but he also feels guilt over most of his line-crossing actions, is capable of getting along with just about anyone under the right circumstances, and genuinely cares about his job as an educator. The Atoner: Gamby genuinely feels remorse for his actions. As misguided as he is, he really does try to make things better (well, as much as he can) during season two. After seeing the life he doomed a drug-dealing student toby framing him and getting him expelled,Gamby relents and re-admits him. Bad Liar: In sharp contrast to Russell, Gamby is terrible at covering for himself.

characters.name: Gamby
characters.description: Co-vice principal of North Jackson High, Gamby is chief disciplinarian of the school. He has allied with his nemesis and fellow vice principal, Lee Russell, to take down new principal Dr. Belinda Brown. Gamby’s ultimate goal is to get the job himself. A Lighter Shade of Black: Gamby would be much harder to sympathize with if it weren't for Russell being so much worse of a person than him. Anti-Villain: Gamby is selfish, stupid and narrow-minded, but he also feels guilt over most of his line-crossing actions, is capable of getting along with just about anyone under the right circumstances, and genuinely cares about his job as an educator. The Atoner: Gamby genuinely feels remorse for his actions. As misguided as he is, he really does try to make things better (well, as much as he can) during season two. After seeing the life he doomed a drug-dealing student toby framing him and getting him expelled,Gamby relents and re-admits him. Bad Liar: In sharp contrast to Russell, Gamby is terrible at covering for himself.

characters.name: Russell
characters.description: Russell works alongside Neal Gamby as one of North Jackson High’s vice principals. He takes a more two-faced approach to undermining new principal, Dr. Belinda Brown. Willing to ally with Gamby to clear the way, Russell hopes to eventually sit in the principal’s chair himself. Ambiguously Bi: He's a catty, campy fellow who favors bow ties and sashays everywhere he goes. During a shared hallucination between him and Gamby, he gives a Judas kiss to another man before killing him. In-universe he is perceived as being in aTransparent Closet. Despite this, he does seem to genuinely love his wife, is clearly attracted to women, and there is no evidence he has had any gay relationships. Bad Boss: Becomes an absolute tyrant after being named temporary principal of North Jackson at the beginning of season 2, running it much more like a dictatorship than a school. The entire staff hates his guts. Big Ego, Hidden Depths: Russell's enormous ego masks a cripplingly insecure and emasculated man. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: In Season 1. As a Vice Principal, Russell is highly popular among the teaching staff due to his witty, outgoing and thoughtful personality. It'sallan act: he is contemptuous of far more than just Gamby. He's a vindictive, cruel man who's created intensive files on everyone in the school. His true colors come to light when the staff start treating him differently because he's the boss and he can't make them like him any more. If he can't be loved, he decides to be feared.

characters.name: Dr. Brown
characters.description: The mother of two boys, Dr. Belinda Brown is the new principal of North Jackson High School. A recent Philadelphia transplant and Berkeley graduate, Dr. Brown is no-nonsense, and takes great pride in her job. Alliterative Name:BelindaBrown. Always Someone Better: Introduced as such to both Gamby and Russel. Neither of them has any hope of competing with her as an educator, and so the only chance that either of them has of stealing the position of principal of North Jackson away from her is through sabotage. Beware the Nice Ones: She's a pleasant and kind woman all around, but she can be scary and vicious when she's angered. Big Bad: AHero Antagonistexample in season 1. Gamby and Russel are both driven throughout the season by their desire to take her down.

characters.name: Ms. Snodgrass
characters.description: Ms. Snodgrass teaches English at North Jackson. Unlike the more rigid Gamby, she prefers to look at students and situations case-by-case when it comes to the rules. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: She's definitely one of the most moral people at the school and she looks rather angelic. Ms. Fanservice: She has some tight-fitting skirts. Not only does Gamby notice this, but so does the camera. Only Friend: Before and after being Neil's love interest, she's the only faculty member who's not completely put off by him. She also is the first to stand up for him against the Sweat Guys. Precision F-Strike: After two episodes of fending off the boorish Gamby with mild polite resistance, Amanda directly tells him he's ruining the field trip and calls him a "Bitch Ass".

characters.name: Dayshawn
characters.description: Dayshawn is in charge of the cafeteria at North Jackson High. He’s Gamby’s eyes and ears at North Jackson, and often encourages him to try to socialize more with the rest of the faculty. The Confidant: To Gamby. And he's really good at it. In fact, he's of the few people who can break through to him. Mistaken for Gay: Dayshawn thinks Gamby's break-up (professional and platonic) is about a gay relationship gone astray. Token Minority: He's treated like this by Gamby in-universe.

characters.name: Nash
characters.description: Eager to please, Nash is the new co-vice principal of North Jackson high school. Her intensity and disciplinary instincts take a page from Gamby's book. Doppelganger: She has the casual cruelty towards students that Russell and Gamby do.

characters.name: Gale
characters.description: Gale is Neal Gamby’s ex-wife, with whom he has an very bitter relationship with. She and Neal have a 13-year-old daughter, Janelle. Gale is now married to Ray Liptrapp.

characters.name: Ray
characters.description: Married to Gamby’s ex-wife, Gale, Ray is much more tolerant of Neal than Neal is of him. Ray is a motocrosser, and has encouraged his stepdaughter, Janelle, to try it out. Cool Dad: Neil's jealousy is that he falls so heavily into this. Nice Guy: Despite "stealing" another man's wife, Ray is a genuinely nice and thoughtful guy who sincerely wants to be a good stepfather to Janelle while respecting Neal. He's never anything less than completely supportive no matter what kind of abuse he takes from Gamby.

characters.name: Ms. Abbott
characters.description: An emotionally unstable civics teacher at North Jackson. Ax-Crazy: She's deeply,deeplyunhinged.Her behavior escalates into violence. Betty and Veronica: Although Gamby cares about 10% as much about Abbott as Snodgress, Abbott does not see things this way. She thinks she can offer a more sexually available, a more wild, and more forward alternative for Gamby. Clingy Jealous Girl: Although it's not entirely clear why she's attracted to Gamby, she is pretty laser-focused on hooking up with him again. She even goes so far as tospike Brian's drink

characters.name: Janelle
characters.description: Neal and Gale’s 13-year-old daughter, Janelle loves her dad. Although she rides horses, she has expressed interest in motocross, like her stepfather Ray. My Horse Is a Motorbike: Invoked as she expresses interest in riding a motorcycle as a replacement for her horses.

characters.name: Hayden
characters.description: Mr. Hayden is a history teacher at North Jackson. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: He's handsome and charming, and his students clearly love him, but he's also rather thoughtless and mean. Cool Teacher: Hayden's students seem to love him due to his good looks and easy charm. Domestic Abuser: To Amanda, although their relationship is short-lived. He doesn't care much for her as a human being and is deeply callous about her feelings for him, ditching her the second another woman gains his interest. Really Gets Around: He moves swiftly on from Amanda to a younger teacher's assistant, implying he's something of a womanizer.

characters.name: Welles
characters.description: The recently retired principal of North Jackson, Welles left to care for his sick wife. Cloudcuckoolander's Minder: All of his scenes are him having to deal with Gamby and Russell acting like petulant children. He even warns them when he's about to turn around again while they had been slapping at each other during the Pledge of Allegiance. Deadpan Snarker: In keeping with being played by Bill Murray, almost all of his dialogue is both deadpan and somewhat snarky.
